$comment: Brand-authorization cross-check conformance vectors for the verify_brand_claim / verify_brand_claims payload-envelope response-signing profile. Each case asserts the authorization decision a verifier MUST make AFTER the JWS signature itself verifies: the signing agent named by the envelope's agent_url MUST be an authorized type:brand agent in the asserted brand_domain's brand.json, and the kid MUST resolve only within that entry's jwks_uri. Independent of cryptographic signing — every case assumes the envelope signature already verified, and tests only the brand.json authorization binding. All entities are fictional. Cited by docs/building/by-layer/L1/security.mdx §Designated-task payload-envelope response signing, Brand-authorization cross-check.

cases.name: forged-owned-for-unowned-brand
cases.rule: agent_url absent from brand_domain's brand.json agents[] -> reject, regardless of a valid signature

cases.name: agent-url-present-but-wrong-type
cases.rule: agents[] entry matches url but type is not 'brand' -> reject (brand-protocol responses are answered by the type:brand agent)

cases.name: trailing-slash-mismatch-is-not-a-match
cases.rule: step 2 uses byte-equality, no canonicalization: a trailing-slash difference is not a match -> reject

cases.name: ambiguous-duplicate-brand-agent-urls
cases.rule: more than one type:brand entry byte-equals agent_url (operator misconfig; agents[] URLs MUST be unique) -> reject ambiguous

cases.name: forged-rejection-from-unbound-agent
cases.rule: rejection direction (not_ours) is also bound: an agent_url absent from brand.json cannot forge a rejection -> reject

cases.name: authorized-brand-agent-accepts
cases.rule: agent_url byte-equals exactly one type:brand agents[] entry -> accept, then resolve kid only within that entry's jwks_uri
